response:
To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix.

Let the matrix be:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} -4 & -13 & 10 \\ -9 & 10 & 4 \\ 12 & -5 & -14 \end{pmatrix} $$

The characteristic polynomial $p(\lambda)$ is defined as:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det \begin{pmatrix} -4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\ -9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\ 12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda \end{pmatrix} $$

### Step 1: Expand the determinant
We can expand the determinant along the first row:
$$ \det(A - \lambda I) = (-4 - \lambda) \begin{vmatrix} 10 - \lambda & 4 \\ -5 & -14 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} - (-13) \begin{vmatrix} -9 & 4 \\ 12 & -14 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} + 10 \begin{vmatrix} -9 & 10 - \lambda \\ 12 & -5 \end{vmatrix} $$

### Step 2: Calculate the $2 \times 2$ minors

**Minor 1:**
$$ \begin{vmatrix} 10 - \lambda & 4 \\ -5 & -14 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} = (10 - \lambda)(-14 - \lambda) - (4)(-5) $$
$$ = -(10 - \lambda)(14 + \lambda) + 20 $$
$$ = -(140 + 10\lambda - 14\lambda - \lambda^2) + 20 $$
$$ = -(-\lambda^2 - 4\lambda + 140) + 20 $$
$$ = \lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 140 + 20 $$
$$ = \lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 120 $$

**
